title: Your First Conversation — About You
description: You meet Marek again. This time he asks about you, your body, colors, and your family.
context: You run into Marek again. He remembers you and wants to get to know you better. Use Block 2 words to talk about yourself, describe people, mention body pain, and talk about family.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks your name. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Volám sa Kai. — perfect introduction. Volám sa is the standard way to give your name.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Volám sa + your name is the correct response when someone asks for your name.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: Nice to meet you! Where are you from?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks where you are from. Pick a correct country-of-origin answer.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Som z... — correct pattern. The country can change, but the structure stays the same.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Som z... + country is the pattern for saying where you are from.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: Are you a man or a woman?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks your gender. Pick whichever applies to you.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Som muž / Som žena — both are correct identity answers depending on the person.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Som muž or Som žena when the question is about gender.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: A aký je váš stav? Ste ženatý, vydatá alebo slobodný?
exchanges.translation: And what is your status? Are you married or single?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks about marital status. Pick a valid status answer.

choices.translation: I am married. (said by a man)
I am married. (said by a woman)

exchanges.correctFeedback: Ženatý, vydatá, and slobodný/slobodná are relationship-status words from Block 2.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: For marital status, use Som ženatý, Som vydatá, or Som slobodný/slobodná.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Ako vyzeráte? Ste vysoký alebo nízky?
exchanges.translation: What do you look like? Are you tall or short?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks about height. Pick a grammatically correct description.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct. Slovak adjectives change ending depending on gender: vysoký/vysoká, nízky/nízka.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Som vysoký/vysoká or Som nízky/nízka to describe height.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: A aké máte vlasy alebo oči?
exchanges.translation: And what hair or eyes do you have?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks for a color description. Pick a correct phrase.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Mám + color + vlasy/oči is the useful color-description pattern.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: For describing yourself, use Mám hnedé vlasy, Mám modré oči, or another color + body part phrase.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Prepáčte, vyzeráte unavene. Bolí vás niečo?
exchanges.translation: Sorry, you look tired. Does something hurt?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if something hurts. Choose the body-pain sentence.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Bolí ma... is the key body-pain pattern from Block 2.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Bolí ma + body part when you need to say what hurts.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Rozumiem. A máte tu partnerku alebo partnera?
exchanges.translation: I understand. And do you have a female or male partner here?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if you have a partner. Pick whichever can be true.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Manželku, manžela, and Nie, nemám are the three natural partner answers.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: The question is about a partner. Use Mám tu manželku, Mám tu manžela, or Nie, nemám.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: And what about parents or siblings?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks about parents and siblings. Pick a correct answer.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Rodičia, brat, sestra, and súrodenci are the core parents-and-siblings words.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: For this question, answer with rodičia, brat, sestra, or súrodenci.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Máte aj starých rodičov, tetu alebo strýka?
exchanges.translation: Do you also have grandparents, an aunt, or an uncle?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks about extended family. Pick the extended-family phrase.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Good. This reviews extended family and in-laws from the Family topic.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use starí rodičia, teta, strýko, or svokrovci for extended-family answers.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Výborne, teraz vás poznám lepšie. Ďakujem, Kai!
exchanges.translation: Excellent, now I know you better. Thank you, Kai!
exchanges.prompt: Marek thanks you for sharing. How do you respond warmly?

choices.translation: Thank you too! Nice to meet you.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Ďakujem aj vám! Teší ma. — warm and natural. You returned the thanks and closed the conversation nicely.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Ďakujem aj vám! Teší ma is the warmest natural reply here.

completionMessage: 🎉 Excellent! You introduced yourself, talked about identity and appearance, used colors and body-pain phrases, and described family in Slovak.
